title: The UNDERUSED Injection Technique that takes YEARS off your patients
filename: The UNDERUSED Injection Technique that takes YEARS off your patients.en.vtt
excerpt: Want to take years off your patients through an injection that's so subtle none of their friends would even know in this episode I'm going to share with you my thinking around one of the most underused injection techniques and why I believe that it can take years off your patient…

segments.text: want to take years off your patients through an injection that's so subtle none of their friends would even know in this episode I'm going to share with you my thinking around one of the most underused injection techniques and why I believe that it can take years off your patients but is underused due to lack of understanding of process technique and Anatomy the reason this is so underused is that patients rarely ask for it you need to be doing a good consultation before you will ever be able to get to understand this procedure but it is actually a really powerful sign of Aging Temple volume loss is seldom seen on The Young and is much more commonly associated with much older faces so improving it makes a dramatic difference in a way that most patients don't consciously perceive the reason for this is it is not a conscious distraction it simply becomes part of the overall appearance of an older face when it goes though it can lift your eyebrow it can decrease the clutter around your eyes and make the eyes much more dominant in the face most important it stops people look looking slightly skeletal which is

often how they'll describe it before they have the right words because patients rarely specifically ask for this procedure you need a good consultation good consultations are built around defining what the patient really wants for example to look fresher or younger and then doing a facial analysis that helps match what you see to the goal they're trying to achieve this will help you and the patient understand the areas of key volume loss that you can correct with your procedures and what is more they'll know exactly why you're doing it to create the result that they're after I've learned from managing large teams of clinicians that when this process isn't followed well it can come across like you're just trying to inject all of the face for no reason you must explain carefully and it must be oriented around your patients goals so who are the key candidates for temple treatment firstly it's a patient who isn't focused on another part of the face they often have a more holistic goal to look fresher younger or better looking you can then guide them to the right treatments on any part of the face while still meeting

their goal secondly they tend to be older patients and Slimmer patients and they should have a slight shadow in the temple when you explain the Aesthetics of the face it's important that they understand and they can see the benefit in softening that shadow once these criteria are met you can start to explain the side effects and the risks and agree with them whether is worth the expenditure to get that result so why is this treatment so good for adding youth back into aging faces the most important reason for me is it is so rare to see in younger patients being able to see too much of the shape and Contour of the skull itself makes patients look by definition skeletal this is often the term that patients themselves will use when seeking treatment even before they've isolated the temple as a cause so as you fix this problem you will see a dramatic Improvement in something that is one of the most powerful signs of aging being able to see the skull through the skin so what injection technique should you use to restore youth in the temple this has become a hot topic of debate recently with many injectors taking strong positions both for and against different techniques we

are largely left to choose between two a needle on bone or a canul type technique more superficially the truth is the best injectors use both techniques and use them appropriately in different patients it's important to also understand some of the more analog variables these are the safety steps that you can take that are harder to measure and debate we are so guilty in Aesthetics of debating needle versus canula that we often forget all the other safety steps that are also included in a safe procedure so what would I do in my average case typically I do prefer the needle on bone technique but I don't just inject the temple there are multiple steps I can do to make it safer the first thing is the angle of the needle and the position of the needle should be placed in such a way as you get maximum benefit for the least amount of volume the defining point of Temple volume loss is the shadow behind the periorbital Rim as you elevate the tissue just here you often with a small amount of product will get a relatively large decrease in the shadow that makes someone look gal but before I inject I'll also be looking in

several different ways for ways of reducing risk for example feel with every patient for a pulse any pulse or even if you think it might be your own finger find another spot where it feels completely still this decreases your chance of injecting through or into a blood vessel the second is you can use an ultrasound which accurately picks up larger vessels quite well in this area and can be used routinely next we can use the negative pressure in the syringe through aspirating to detect intravascular placement or even having passed through a vessel pull back on the syringe just after you've touched the periosteum you can then pull the whole needle in and out to see if you've gone through a blood vessel and maximize the chance of getting that little flashback that tells you you're in a vessel in which case you would stop the procedure and inject somewhere else or if you don't get a positive you know that you've reduced the risk dramatically somewhere between 50 and 80% of being intravascular layer all these steps together and you have dramatically reduced risk the amount of trauma for this technique is also relatively tiny it's one injection Point once you've

done all those steps you place all your product in through that one entry point it is much less likely to cause trauma to nerves blood vessels and veins even if we're not talking about occlusion than using a canula across a wider area so when do I use a canula is a fantastic tool for more superficial or wider spread volume loss so once you've corrected the most defining point of volume loss I often find a canula is useful for a spreading product in those cases that still need more which is a minority of my cases so let's have a look at the shape and Contour of a face that may require Temple volume replacement in this particular case the periorbital Ridge has become a dominant feature defined by loss of fat underneath the per orbital Ridge but also in the temple itself revealing the shape and Contour of the skull given that the patient had a goal of looking overall younger and fresher it was appropriate to recommend treatment in the temple as you can see by the shadow here it's these Shadows here that Define the shape of the skull so anything we do to replace volume

directly in that shadow will make the temple blend in with the more projected area of the temple behind so here is relatively full and then we have the empty space next to it if we add volume in between those we will connect those structures harmonizing the light and the shade so there is a more gradual transition across the lateral part of the head you can see in the after picture how the contrast between the periorbital Ridge and the temple is softened as is the area volume underneath connecting the forehead with the lower lid this makes for a fresher more youthful appearance that looks very natural to all but the most trained injectors if you want to learn more about how to create natural youthful results for your patients consult them well create beautiful treatment plans around a real framework rather than just opinion make sure you click on the link in the description of this video that will tell you more about my training
